meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Overcome Project Deadlock for Students
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to solve project paralysis. A high-level guide for students using Antigravity to build professional-grade AI applications.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project paralysis requires shifting from passive learning to active architecture. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform abstract ideas into production-ready codebases. This setup automates the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on high-level logic and system design, effectively turning 'no ideas' into a portfolio of shipping products.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of ambition. You have the theoretical knowledge, the curiosity, and the drive, yet you find yourself paralyzed by the 'what to build' dilemma. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a failure of workflow. When you don't have a system to rapidly prototype, every project feels like an insurmountable mountain.

We are going to bridge that gap today. By leveraging Claude Code as your autonomous pair programmer and Antigravity as your architectural scaffold, you will move from ideation to deployment in hours, not weeks. This is the engineering rigor that separates the hobbyists from the builders who actually ship.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Configure your CLI environment to ensure Claude Code has full read/write access to your local project directory structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS project structure with a modular backend and clean documentation architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude config set --editor vscode && claude init

steps.title: Architecting the Logic Core
steps.description: Define the project scope using Antigravity to generate a high-level system diagram before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a system architecture document for an AI-powered task management tool with a focus on scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this architecture document and suggest three optimizations for database performance.'

steps.title: Automated Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the core services based on your Antigravity architecture, ensuring type safety and modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate for a TypeScript backend with Express, including best practices for error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scaffold the backend services based on the architecture defined in the /docs folder.'

steps.title: Contextual Iteration
steps.description: Feed specific feature requests into Claude Code, utilizing the context from your Antigravity files to maintain consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust integration test suite for the user authentication service created in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the authentication logic and write unit tests for the login endpoint.'

steps.title: Refactoring and Optimization
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final code review, identifying potential technical debt or performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Conduct a security and performance audit on the codebase, highlighting areas for optimization.

pro_tips: Always maintain a /docs/arch.md file; Claude Code uses this as the 'source of truth' to prevent feature creep.
Use 'claude ask' to explain complex legacy patterns before attempting to refactor them.
Configure your .claudeignore file strictly to avoid token wastage on large node_modules or build artifacts.
Treat Claude Code as a junior developer; always verify the generated code with a secondary manual review.
When stuck, use Antigravity to generate a 'decision log' to document why specific architectural choices were made.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, push your repository to GitHub, document the 'Why' in your README, and share the link on LinkedIn to build your professional reputation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to turn any spark of an idea into a functional, portfolio-ready application. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start building the one you have right now. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
